mnemonics: CHOMSKY'S HIERARCHY: Regular (Type-3), Context-Free (Type-2), Context-Sensitive (Type-1), Recursively Enumerable (Type-0). Remember the mnemonic 'Really Cool Cats Sometimes Roar' for the order.
keyPoints: The Chomsky hierarchy classifies formal grammars and their corresponding languages and automata.
Turing Machines recognize recursively enumerable languages and define the limits of mechanical computation.
A language is decidable if a Turing Machine exists that halts on every input, accepting or rejecting it.
commonMistakes: Assuming a language is context-free if it requires a pushdown automaton; some non-context-free languages can be recognized by more powerful automata.
Believing a Turing Machine that never halts on some inputs proves a language is undecidable; the definition requires a machine that halts on *every* input.
